name: what-if-serious-scientific-answers-to-absurd-hypothetical-questions
description: Randall Munroe's What If? — a scientific thinking and Fermi estimation toolkit answering absurd hypothetical questions with real physics, chemistry, and biology, teaching scientific literacy through curiosity, humor, and approximation.
useCase: Learn scientific thinking through absurd hypotheticals — master Fermi estimation, risk assessment, and the joy of curiosity-driven science.
knownRisks: The book prioritizes entertainment over depth — it is not a physics textbook
Some scenarios are deliberately shocking (relativistic baseball = nuclear explosion)
The 'back-of-envelope' method has limits — not all problems can be approximated
